Other: 8AM: Standing for a sail in sight to the Ed. At 8:30 fired a blank cartridge to make the vessel in sight show her colors. At 8:45 she showed American colors, and we sent a boat to board her. Found her to be the ship Erie of New York, without papers, or any person claiming to be captain, and with 893 slaves on board, having a mixed crew of Spaniards, Americans and Menchineans. (?) Making preparations to send her to Monrovia with a prize crew.
Noon: Lieut Dunnington and Passed Mid'm Todd with a crew of ten men went on board the slaver to take possession. All the prize's men, save three, on board the Mohican.

Note: We got underway some time in this watch.
Other: 1600: The prize got underway with her head to the Nd. We steamed around her, gave three cheers, which were returned by the prize, and started on our course.
